message:
feat(app,fs,process): add deinit lifecycle functions and comprehensive unit tests

Add proper resource cleanup lifecycle to three native-only modules:

- app.h/app.c: add asx_app_deinit() declaration and implementation
- fs.h/fs.c: add asx_fs_deinit() declaration and implementation
- process.h/process.c: add asx_process_deinit() declaration and
  implementation
- lifecycle.c: integrate deinit calls into runtime shutdown sequence

Tests:
- test_app.c: comprehensive app lifecycle tests
- test_fs.c: comprehensive filesystem lifecycle tests
- test_process.c: comprehensive process lifecycle tests

- Update profile capability matrix documentation
- Update beads issue tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-56t.3","title":"Create certification-ready evidence bundle skeletons and mapping templates","description":"Background:
Safety-critical and regulated adopters require structured evidence linking claims to artifacts.

Subtasks:
- Define bundle structure for invariants, tests, conformance, and gate evidence.
- Define mapping templates for standards-oriented documentation (for example ISO-style traceability needs).
- Link bundle sections to traceability index and CI artifact IDs.
- Include canonical pointers to unit/invariant/e2e structured logs, run manifests, parity diffs, and replay bundles.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Bundle skeleton is complete enough for audited release candidates.
- Mappings are explicit and reproducible from repository artifacts.
- No claim exists without a documented evidence location.
- Evidence bundle is sufficient for third-party audit replay of key failures and mitigations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:14.307747108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T06:05:46.811955563Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T06:05:46.811937038Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["certification","evidence","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-56t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-296.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-296.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-296.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-56t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-56t.4","title":"Define C API/ABI stability contract and versioned compatibility process","description":"Background:
C API/ABI stability is adoption-critical, especially for embedded integrators and long-lived deployments.

Subtasks:
- Define public API versioning macros (`ASX_API_VERSION_MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH`) and compatibility policy.
- Define symbol-visibility and header hygiene rules for static/dynamic portability.
- Define config-struct evolution rules (size-field pattern vs frozen structs).
- Implement consumer-shim CI job that compiles against public headers only.
- Define deprecation/migration documentation requirements and release checklist integration.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Stability contract is explicit, versioned, and visible to integrators.
- ABI break detection/check process is enforceable in CI/release workflow.
- Breaking-change process and migration notes requirements are unambiguous.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:20.138071538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:38:29.200797537Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:38:29.200777930Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","api","compatibility","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-56t.4","depends_on_id":"bd-296.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.4","depends_on_id":"bd-56t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.4","depends_on_id":"bd-56t.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.4","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.4","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-56t.5","title":"Run API ergonomics validation gate with compilable usage vignettes","description":"Background:
Plan section 7.3.2 adds a pre-lock API usability gate to avoid shipping semantically-correct but hostile C APIs.

Subtasks:
- Implement 3-5 compilable vignette programs covering lifecycle, obligations, budgets, replay, and freestanding hooks.
- Measure boilerplate/footgun burden and discoverability per vignette.
- Propose header/API adjustments where ergonomics are unnecessarily poor.
- Record final ergonomics decision and rationale in API docs before header freeze.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Vignettes compile and run in CI against public headers.
- Ergonomics findings are documented with actionable outcomes.
- Public header freeze cannot proceed without explicit ergonomics sign-off.","notes":"Work completed in branch/worktree (executed under bd-66l lane): added test-vignettes make target and CI integration, added API ergonomics validation doc sign-off, implemented/fixed vignette programs for lifecycle/obligations/budgets/replay/hooks. rch validation: test-vignettes/build/format-check/lint/test pass. Conformance now executes under rch with SEMANTIC_DELTA_ARTIFACT_DIR override support in tools/ci/run_conformance.sh; remaining fail is existing semantic-delta non-budgetable condition.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:05.468931266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T23:14:47.201678089Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T23:14:47.201657571Z","close_reason":"Completed: final obligations vignette fix landed; validation gates pass via rch (build, format-check, lint, test, test-invariants, conformance).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption","api","docs","ergonomics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-56t.5","depends_on_id":"bd-296.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.5","depends_on_id":"bd-56t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-56t.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-5p3t","title":"Self-gate filesystem header for browser builds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-24T00:39:35.099817283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T00:41:50.396229379Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T00:41:50.395588155Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-66l","title":"EPIC: Hard Quality Gates and CI Enforcement","description":"Background:
The plan defines non-negotiable gates. They must be automated and enforced to prevent regressions from entering mainline.

Goal:
Encode all required gates in CI with reproducible artifacts and clear fail reasons.

Gates include:
- portability, OOM semantics, fuzz parity
- embedded matrix, profile parity, semantic-delta budget
- HFT tail/jitter, automotive deadline/watchdog
- crash/restart replay continuity
- warning/static-analysis/bounded-proof gates

Why this serves users:
- transforms policy into enforceable guarantees
- makes trust scalable beyond individual reviewers.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","notes":"Executed API ergonomics validation lane work (bd-56t.5 content) under this quality-gate epic: added make test-vignettes target, CI step+manifest wiring, tests/TEST.md vignette coverage docs, docs/API_ERGONOMICS_VALIDATION.md sign-off, and fixed vignette compile/runtime issues. Validation via rch: make test-vignettes/build/format-check/lint/test pass. Conformance now runs under rch with SEMANTIC_DELTA_ARTIFACT_DIR override support in tools/ci/run_conformance.sh; current remote conformance still fails on existing non-budgetable semantic-delta condition (fail=1, diff_records=1), not a permission failure.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"IvoryGlacier","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.390018086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:30:49.559586930Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:30:49.559560691Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","phase-10","quality-gates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-296","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66l.1","title":"Implement CI workflow matrix for build, tests, conformance, profiles, fuzz, embedded, and perf","description":"Background:
Quality policy is only real when encoded in CI. Workflow structure must mirror plan-gate structure.

Subtasks:
- Define jobs for check, unit-invariant, conformance, profile-parity, fuzz-parity, embedded-matrix, perf-tail-deadline, and e2e scenario packs.
- Ensure deterministic artifact naming and retention.
- Ensure failure output is actionable and machine-readable where possible.
- Enforce publication of test run manifests and first-failure summaries for each test lane.

Acceptance Criteria:
- CI runs all required jobs on expected triggers.
- Job outputs are sufficient for triage without rerunning everything locally.
- No critical gate is left as manual-only.
- Missing e2e artifacts/manifests fail the workflow.","notes":"2026-02-28: Completed CI workflow hardening for lane manifests/first-failure summaries and deterministic artifact naming. Updated .github/workflows/ci.yml and perf.yml: added per-lane machine-readable manifest emission (build/ci-manifests/*.manifest.json), e2e run_manifest publication enforcement, conformance/profile summary presence checks, fuzz/embedded artifact validation, lint-docs in check lane, deterministic artifact names using github.run_id/run_attempt. Verification via rch offload: make format-check, lint, build, test, conformance, profile-parity all PASS.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:00.106194478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T01:21:14.302625020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T01:21:14.302597949Z","close_reason":"Implemented CI/perf workflow matrix refinements, deterministic artifact naming, lane manifest + first-failure summaries, and manifest/artifact enforcement checks.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","gates","workflows"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66l.10","title":"Implement section 10.7 warning/static-analysis/bounded-model gates","description":"Background:
Plan section 10.7 adds mandatory gates replacing lost Rust compiler guarantees in core kernel paths.

Subtasks:
- Enforce warning-as-error policy across GCC/Clang/MSVC with curated high-signal flags.
- Add static-analyzer job and triage policy (must-fix/waiver/backlog) wired into CI outcomes.
- Add bounded model-check job for small-state transition/cancellation/double-resolve properties.
- Publish rerun commands and artifact expectations for each gate in CI docs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Section 10.7 gates run automatically and fail correctly on violations.
- Analyzer waivers are explicit, justified, and auditable.
- Bounded-model artifacts are reproducible and attached to failing runs.","notes":"Section 10.7 gates implemented: (1) tools/ci/run_static_analysis.sh — cppcheck + clang-tidy with ASX_ANALYZER_WAIVER support, JSON reporting; (2) tests/invariant/model_check/test_bounded_model.c — 19 tests over 10 invariants (terminal, forward progress, cancel monotonicity, obligation linearity, spawn prohibition, table completeness, terminal predicates, cancel severity, state names, reachability); (3) Makefile targets: lint-static-analysis, model-check; (4) CI integration in .github/workflows/ci.yml","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:24:59.775986492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T03:21:14.253538281Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T03:21:14.253468431Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","formal","lint","static-analysis"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-296.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-66l.8","title":"Implement per-target binary-size and cold-start SLO gates","description":"Background:
Idea round 2 includes per-target binary size and cold-start gates as embedded adoption multipliers.

Subtasks:
- Capture size and cold-start baselines for key target profiles and triplets.
- Define budget policy and tolerance windows per profile/resource class.
- Integrate checks into CI with clear delta reporting and reproducible rerun commands.
- Emit structured gate artifacts (target, baseline, delta, suspect modules, run metadata) for trend analysis and release review.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Regressions in size/start metrics fail gate when outside approved budgets.
- Reports identify changed targets and likely contributing modules.
- Baseline updates require explicit rationale and audit trail.
- Gate artifacts are machine-readable and linkable from release evidence bundles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.423164995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:29:18.543969085Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:29:18.543951361Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","embedded","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66l.9","title":"Enforce per-bead evidence linkage contract for unit/e2e/logging completeness","description":"Background:
The hard-part verification rules require every semantic-sensitive implementation bead to carry explicit proof linkage, not only CI-level gating.

Subtasks:
- Define required evidence fields per implementation bead: linked unit tests, linked e2e scripts, expected artifact manifests, and replay/parity references.
- Require linkage against the unit/invariant completeness matrix (bd-1md.12) and canonical e2e suite contract (bd-1md.10) so evidence remains coverage-complete, not ad hoc.
- Add validation tooling that checks bead metadata and notes for required evidence references before status transitions to done.
- Integrate checks with anti-butchering proof-block gate and traceability index updates.
- Provide reviewer/operator guidance for interpreting evidence packs quickly.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Semantic-sensitive beads cannot be marked complete without linked unit/e2e/logging evidence.
- Evidence packs reference explicit unit/invariant obligations and at least one canonical e2e lane per covered semantic surface.
- Validation failures are explicit and actionable.
- Evidence links remain machine-checkable for audit and release workflows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralValley","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:18.357456678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T03:44:38.432684259Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T03:44:38.432665053Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","quality-gates","testing","traceability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-296","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-296.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.9","depends_on_id":"bd-66  {"id":"bd-aw69","title":"EPIC: Embedded Target Matrix Gate (GATE-EMBEDDED)","description":"## Goal
Implement GATE-EMBEDDED: cross-compilation for ARM and RISC-V targets with QEMU-based smoke
tests, validating that the C port builds and runs correctly on constrained embedded platforms.

## Why This Matters
The C port's raison d'être is portability to embedded targets that Rust's async runtime cannot
reach (bare-metal MCUs, router-class devices, automotive ECUs). Without actually building and
testing on these targets, the "portable ANSI C" claim is aspirational rather than proven.

GATE-EMBEDDED is one of the 9 non-negotiable quality gates from AGENTS.md:
"Embedded target matrix gate (cross-build + QEMU + device smoke)."

The three resource classes (R1=tight, R2=balanced, R3=roomy) defined in asx_config.h are
specifically designed for embedded deployment. The FREESTANDING and EMBEDDED_ROUTER profiles
compile with no libc dependency. This gate proves those profiles work end-to-end.

## What "Done" Looks Like
- Cross-compilation succeeds for: arm-none-eabi (Cortex-M), aarch64-linux-gnu, riscv32-unknown-elf
- QEMU user-mode runs the C test suite for aarch64 and riscv32 targets
- Bare-metal builds (arm-none-eabi) link successfully with FREESTANDING profile
- Resource class R1 builds fit within 64KB RAM budget
- make embedded-matrix passes in CI

## Architectural Context
The Makefile already has profile-parity targets that build with ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING and
ASX_PROFILE_EMBEDDED_ROUTER. The freestanding hooks (src/platform/freestanding/hooks.c) provide
stub implementations for platforms without libc. What's missing is the actual cross-compiler
toolchains, QEMU setup, and the CI integration to exercise them.

## Risk
- Cross-compiler toolchain installation varies by host OS
- QEMU user-mode may not perfectly emulate all target quirks
- Bare-metal linking requires a linker script and startup code
- R1 memory budget (64KB) may require tuning ASX_MAX_* constants","notes":"## CRITICAL: EXISTING INFRASTRUCTURE AUDIT (2026-03-12)

The following ALREADY EXISTS and must NOT be recreated or modified:
- tools/ci/run_embedded_matrix.sh: Linux-musl cross-build matrix (armv7, aarch64, mipsel)
- tools/ci/run_qemu_smoke.sh: QEMU testing for Linux-musl targets
- make ci-embedded-matrix: Makefile target for Linux-musl embedded builds
- tools/ci/generate_layout_budget_report.sh: Binary size/layout reporting
- tools/ci/compute_layout_budget_delta.sh: Cross-run regression detection

## What This Epic ACTUALLY Adds (COMPLEMENTARY, NOT REPLACEMENT)
Bare-metal cross-compilation for FREESTANDING/EMBEDDED_ROUTER profiles on:
- ARM Cortex-M (arm-none-eabi-gcc) — new toolchain
- RISC-V (riscv-elf-gcc) — new toolchain
- QEMU semihosting for bare-metal binaries — new execution mode
- R1 memory budgets for .text/.bss sections — new measurements

## Subtask Inventory (8 total)
.1 Install bare-metal ARM toolchain (60m) — Linux-musl ARM already exists
.2 Install RISC-V toolchain (60m) — genuinely new
.3 QEMU semihosting setup (90m) — Linux user-mode QEMU already exists
.4 Bare-metal Makefile targets (120m) — Linux-musl targets already exist
.5 Bare-metal link validation (120m) — genuinely new
.6 R1 memory budgets (90m) — layout reporting exists for musl
.7 CI gate for bare-metal (120m) — embedded CI exists for musl
.8 QEMU e2e tests for bare-metal (180m) — QEMU smoke exists for musl","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":840,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:06:25.215072286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:39:35.547567356Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:39:35.546118414Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embedded","phase3","portability"]}

 {"id":"bd-fjer","title":"Fix combinator deadline leak and quorum drain semantics","description":"Three semantic bugs in combinators: (1) timeout/retry_timeout never disarm deadlines from timer wheel after completing, leaking timer resources; (2) quorum combinator marks loser branches done=1 without calling branch_cancel_after_final_poll(), violating the cancellation contract that all other combinators (race, select) honor; (3) race_timeout can double-drain losers when timeout and winner declaration coincide.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T08:05:18.792349514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:08:25.444376306Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:08:25.443695968Z","close_reason":"Fixed all three combinator semantic bugs: deadline disarm on timeout/retry_timeout, quorum drain with cooperative final poll, race_timeout winner-vs-timeout priority. 134 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["combinator","correctness"]}

 {"id":"bd-hwb","title":"EPIC: Core Semantics and Guarantee Substitution in ANSI C/C99","description":"Background:
Rust compile-time guarantees are absent in C. We must reconstitute equivalent runtime/proof guarantees via explicit mechanisms.

Goal:
Implement asx_core semantic primitives and safety scaffolding with deterministic failure behavior.

Core obligations:
- explicit state authorities
- generation-safe handles and ownership boundaries
- ghost monitors and linearity checks
- strict resource exhaustion semantics
- canonical error taxonomy and stability policy

Why this serves users:
- correctness under pressure for embedded systems
- predictable failure semantics for high-stakes systems
- confidence that performance tuning does not erase guarantees.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.038789705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:59:25.155834562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:59:25.155810537Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","phase-3","phase-4","safety","semantics"]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.1","title":"Implement core API types: status, errors, outcomes, budgets, cancellation, and IDs","description":"Background:
These primitives are shared across all runtime behavior and therefore must be stable, deterministic, and audit-friendly.

Subtasks:
- Define status/error taxonomy with stable code families.
- Define outcome types and ordering semantics.
- Define budget and cancellation core types with explicit failure semantics.
- Define ID formats and generation strategy for handle safety.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Types are fully documented and covered by unit tests.
- Error/status semantics are deterministic and profile-agnostic.
- Public API naming and visibility are consistent.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:09.812710740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:10:04.652897529Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:10:04.652875979Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","core","phase-3","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb.10","title":"Implement protothread-style task poll contract and region-arena captured-state safety","description":"Background:
Plan sections 6.8.A and 7.4 require a safe C replacement for async/await that preserves lifecycle semantics without stack-lifetime footguns.

Subtasks:
- Implement coroutine-style task macros (`ASX_CO_BEGIN`, `ASX_CO_YIELD`, `ASX_CO_END`) with explicit resume state.
- Enforce region-arena ownership of captured task state; no malloc-owned suspended state in kernel profiles.
- Add explicit yield-safety lint gate that flags non-const locals/raw internal pointers persisting across yield unless waived.
- Define spawn contract for state sizing/finalization so suspended tasks cannot leak on region close.
- Add tests for suspend/resume ordering, cancellation checkpoints, and region-close cleanup.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Poll contract is deterministic and fixture-tested.
- No leaked task state on cancellation or region closure.
- Yield-safety violations are caught automatically by CI lint/static checks.
- Macro workflow is documented with explicit do/dont examples.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:56:45.639588066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:08:14.924460519Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:08:14.924440612Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","coroutines","hard-part"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hwb.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
